title: The Journal of Prompt-Engineered (Moral) Philosophy Or: Why AI-Assisted Ethics Research Requires Process Transparency
abstract: arXiv:2511.08639v5 Announce Type: replace-cross  Abstract: Existing AI disclosure mandates in scholarship require that AI assistance be reported but leave transparency philosophically unspecified: they fix the duty without explaining what the duty serves. We argue that ethical inquiry is essentially contested at two independent levels -- about what it is, and about what it demands of the inquirer -- defeating output-only evaluation and welfare-economic dismissal of the transparency question, and, by extension, reproducibility framings imported from the empirical sciences. The transparency duty is grounded instead in agent-integrity: the legibility, before a community of inquiry, of the identity-constituting commitments that the author's mode of philosophising expresses. Because the standards for evaluating such work are not communally settled, the achievable goal for transparency is not evaluation against agreed criteria but tracking -- accumulating the evidentiary record that lets eac

Title: The Journal of Prompt-Engineered (Moral) Philosophy Or: Why AI-Assisted Ethics Research Requires Process Transparency
Abstract: arXiv:2511.08639v5 Announce Type: replace-cross  Abstract: Existing AI disclosure mandates in scholarship require that AI assistance be reported but leave transparency philosophically unspecified: they fix the duty without explaining what the duty serves. translated_title: 提示工程（道德）哲学期刊：或：为何AI辅助伦理研究需要过程透明
translated_abstract: arXiv:2511.08639v5 公告类型：替换交叉 摘要：现有学术界的AI披露要求规定需报告AI辅助情况，但未从哲学上明确透明度的内涵：它们设定了义务，却未解释该义务服务于何种目的。我们认为，伦理探究在两个独立层面上存在本质争议——即伦理是什么，以及它对探究者有何要求——这否定了仅基于输出结果的评估方式、福利经济学对透明度问题的轻视，以及从经验科学引入的可重复性框架。透明度义务的根基在于行动者完整性：即在探究共同体面前，作者哲学化方式所表达的身份构成性承诺的可读性。由于此类工作的评估标准尚未在共同体层面达成共识，透明度的可实现目标并非依据一致标准进行评估，而是追踪——积累证据记录，使每个共同体成员能自行判断该探究是否可信、是否值得参与。
tldr: 本文提出AI辅助伦理研究中的透明度要求应基于“行动者完整性”概念，即通过过程透明让研究者身份构成性承诺可被共同体追踪和评判，而非依赖预设标准或输出结果。
en_tdlr: This paper argues that transparency in AI-assisted ethics research should be grounded in "agent-integrity," enabling the community to track and judge the identity-constituting commitments expressed through the researcher's mode of philosophizing, rather than relying on predefined criteria or output-only evaluation.
